Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and high chloride. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible renal dysfunction and/or urinary tract pathology. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent hypoglycemia. Recommend further evaluation for renal disease, urinary tract infection, and assessment of glycemic status.
